items.title: Indigenous families want 'crisis' investigated after 15 deaths in six months

children.content: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

gallery.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

thumbnail.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: Remote community love story told through first-time director's rom-com

children.content: In a First Nations romantic comedy debuting in Sydney this week, a new writer-director has used her lived experience to tell the story of young love in a remote Northern Territory community.

image.altText: A young Indigenous woman looks over her left shoulder towards a shirtless man standing behind her.

items.title: Marilyn's world 'fell apart' with one post. What happened next helped her heal

children.content: Marilyn was just five when she first encountered grief. Now 18, the teenager says she's been to more funerals than birthday parties.

image.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

items.title: Famous outback race claims another life after serious motorbike crash

children.content: A motorbike rider has died in the Central Australian outback after a Finke Desert Race crash, with the two-day event expected to continue as police investigate.

gallery.altText: A row of motorbikes at the starting line, their tyres are kicking up clumps of the dirt track.

thumbnail.altText: A row of motorbikes at the starting line, their tyres are kicking up clumps of the dirt track.

items.title: 'Girl who screams most nights': NT hearings address child protection system

children.content: Some foster carers have voiced support for the NT's proposed changes to child protection laws, but others say the current legislation already outlines individual safety as the paramount consideration.

image.altText: Nine people sitting along a long table, each with microphones before them, facing another table where politicians are sitting.

items.title: Biker wants a wife: How a viral post helped a pair find love on the Finke Desert Race track

children.content: When motorbike enthusiast Ben Rawcliffe posted on a Finke Desert Race Facebook group looking for his future wife, his post garnered hundreds of comments. Now, he's back in the red centre, supporting his new partner as she takes to the tough off-road track.

image.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

items.title: Undersea volcano inundates island with 'desert' of stone

children.content: Residents are concerned about access to food and fresh water as pumice stone makes travel difficult for Papua New Guinea's seafaring communities.

A cloud of white smoke steams up from under a deep blue ocean

items.title: Trump storms out of interview after grilling on 'crooked' election claims

children.content: US President Donald Trump angrily storms out of an interview with American broadcaster NBC after he was questioned about his unfounded claims of election fraud.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and red tie, sitting for an interview

items.title: UK, France and Germany back Zelenskyy's call for meeting with Putin

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has received the backing of the leaders of the UK, France and Germany in calling for direct ceasefire talks with Russia.

image.altText: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer, France's President Emmanuel Macron, Ukrain'e President Volodymyr Zelenskyy pose for photo

items.title: It's an AI behemoth. Now Anthropic is making a power play in Australia

children.content: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could cause more harm.

articles.title: Anthropic wants caution but the market wants more AI

items.title: When a detective told a witness Joanna was pregnant, she took police to VCAT

children.content: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

image.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

items.title: Top neurologists say regulator ignored warnings as weed killer decision looms

children.content: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: A man wearing dark jumper and beige puffer coat speaks, seen from behind the shoulder of someone wearing purple

items.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan decries sexist Melbourne billboard

children.content: Premier Jacinta Allan has been joined by Anthony Albanese, Julia Gillard and other politicians — except for Pauline Hanson — in denouncing the language used to describe her on political signs seen in the Melbourne CBD.

gallery.altText: A billboard saying Ditch the Witch

thumbnail.altText: A billboard saying Ditch the Witch

items.title: Ignoring One Nation like 'ignoring will of people', Basil Zempilas warns

children.content: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas says One Nation deserves "respect" and he is open to finding a way of working with the right-wing populist party as support for it grows.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas stands at a podium and addresses the crowd

items.title: History quiz: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?

children.content: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

image.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

items.title: Secrets of Indigenous astronomy revealed in new exhibition

children.content: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of Torres Strait Islander artist Brian Robinson in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

items.title: With his pet chihuahua and trusty gopher, 97yo grazier won't slow down

children.content: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

image.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him

items.title: Obscene acts charges prompt calls for more CCTV

children.content: A woman who reported a terrifying incident while walking at night in Derby says more CCTV cameras would increase safety and help monitor popular locations.

image.altText: The main strip of Derby, a narrow road surrounded by red dirt and bushy trees.

children.content: The court has heard the accused man threatened to expose an affair his former partner allegedly had with the married high-profile Queenslander in 2017.

children.content: Fallen powerlines electrified a metal fence encircling the home before a gas cylinder "went off like a rocket" as a house in Warilla is engulfed within minutes.

items.title: Korda wins US Women's Open title with perilous final putt

children.content: Nelly Korda claims the US Women's Open for her second consecutive major victory, holding off Charley Hull and Gaby Lopez by one shot in Los Angeles.

image.altText: Nelly Korda holds the US Women's Open trophy.

items.title: Perth actor wants police to pay after apartment 'destroyed' during murder probe

children.content: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants in 2012. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

items.title: How this Indigenous mother paved the way for her kids to study medicine

children.content: Ieesha, Abdul and Jamaal Nasser are following in the footsteps of their mother Tatum Maybir, who entered medicine aged 45 after raising her six children.

items.title: Crossbench MPs demand answers from Tasmanian premier, issue ultimatum

children.content: Seven crossbench MPs have written to Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff to demand answers about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's resignation from the ministry, giving him until Friday morning to provide "direct and exact answers".

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff and Madeleine Ogilvie in parliament.

items.title: Outback cake decorator stops reporting vandalism because 'nothing gets done'

children.content: After three vandalism incidents in three years, cake decorator Ishani Harney says the emotional and financial toll of crime lingers long after the damage is repaired.

gallery.altText: Mount Isa cake decorator Ishani Harney holds a shard of glass from her shopfront window that was broken during a 2023 break-in.
